snowflake:position-deletes:v2.notes: Snowflake fully supports reading and writing position deletes for Iceberg v2 tables. For externally managed tables, merge-on-read with positional delete files is the default write mode (ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='AUTO'). For Snowflake-managed v2 tables, copy-on-write is the default but merge-on-read can be enabled via the ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR parameter. Write support for externally managed tables (including positional deletes) is generally available as of October 2025.
caveats: Externally managed v2 tables: merge-on-read with positional deletes is the default (ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='AUTO')
Snowflake-managed v2 tables: copy-on-write by default; set ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='ENABLED' to produce positional delete files
ENABLE_ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ is a deprecated fallback; use ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR instead
Write support for externally managed tables (GA October 2025) on S3, Azure, and Google Cloud

links.label: Write support for externally managed tables (GA Oct 2025)
Write support for externally managed tables (docs)

snowflake:position-deletes:v3.notes: Deletion vectors replace position deletes in V3, providing improved write performance. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
V3 uses deletion vectors instead of position delete files

snowflake:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Row-level equality deletes are not supported. Snowflake uses copy-on-write for all DELETE operations on managed tables.
caveats: Row-level equality deletes not supported; Snowflake uses copy-on-write for DELETE operations

snowflake:equality-deletes:v3.notes: Equality deletes replaced by deletion vectors in V3. Snowflake uses deletion vectors instead.
caveats: V3 uses deletion vectors instead of equality delete files

snowflake:merge-on-read:v2.notes: Merge-on-read is supported for all Iceberg v2 tables in Snowflake, controlled by the ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR parameter. The default value 'AUTO' enables MoR for all externally managed tables and uses copy-on-write for Snowflake-managed v2 tables. Set to 'ENABLED' to force MoR for Snowflake-managed v2 tables. Uses positional delete files for v2. Write support for externally managed tables is generally available as of October 2025. ENABLE_ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ is deprecated in favour of ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR.
caveats: ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='AUTO' (default): MoR for all externally managed tables; CoW for Snowflake-managed v2 tables
Set ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='ENABLED' to force MoR for Snowflake-managed v2 tables
ENABLE_ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ session parameter is deprecated — use ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR instead
Snowflake uses heuristics (row %, file size) to decide per-file whether to use MoR or CoW even when MoR is active

links.label: Write support for externally managed tables (GA Oct 2025)
Write support for externally managed tables (docs)

snowflake:merge-on-read:v3.notes: Deletion vectors supported for improved write performance in Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

links.label: Write to tables by using deletion vectors

snowflake:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Copy-on-write is the default write strategy for Snowflake-managed Iceberg v2 tables. All DELETE, UPDATE, and MERGE operations rewrite affected data files. Merge-on-read is also available (and is the default for externally managed v2 tables) via the ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR parameter.
caveats: Default write strategy for Snowflake-managed v2 tables
All DML operations (DELETE, UPDATE, MERGE) rewrite entire data files when using CoW
Set ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='DISABLED' to force CoW for all tables including externally managed

snowflake:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Copy-on-write continues to be supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Full schema evolution supported for Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables, including add, drop, rename, and reorder columns.
caveats: Schema evolution applies to Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables
Externally managed tables have schema controlled by the external catalog

snowflake:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Full schema evolution supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values are a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:column-default-values:v3.notes: Column default values supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

links.label: Use default values with Iceberg tables

snowflake:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Nanosecond-precision timestamps supported in Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Snowflake supports all Iceberg v2 partition transforms (year, month, day, hour, bucket, truncate, identity) for both Snowflake-managed and externally managed tables. Partitioned writes are GA (Oct 2025). As of Feb 2026 (Preview), Snowflake also supports hierarchical (Hive-style) path layout via PATH_LAYOUT=HIERARCHICAL, enabling compatibility with external engines that expect directory-per-partition layouts.
caveats: Partitioned writes (GA October 2025): all Iceberg v2 transforms supported for managed and externally managed tables
Hierarchical (Hive-style) path layout: PATH_LAYOUT=HIERARCHICAL parameter (Preview Feb 2026)
Default flat file layout used when PATH_LAYOUT is not specified
Bucket partition transform may negatively impact query performance in Snowflake (proprietary micro-partition pruning used instead)

links.label: Hierarchical path layout for partitioned writes (Preview Feb 2026)

snowflake:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Hidden partitioning supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
Bucket partition transform may still impact performance

snowflake:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Partition evolution is supported for externally managed Iceberg tables — Snowflake's partitioned write support (GA October 2025) writes data conforming to the table's current Iceberg partition spec, so changing the spec in your external catalog is reflected in new writes from Snowflake. For Snowflake-managed tables, partition schemes are managed via ALTER ICEBERG TABLE … PARTITION BY; old data retains its existing layout while new data uses the updated spec, following Iceberg's native partition evolution model.
caveats: Partitioned writes (GA October 2025) enable Snowflake to correctly write to tables whose partition spec has evolved
Snowflake-managed tables: use ALTER ICEBERG TABLE … PARTITION BY to evolve the partition spec; data is not automatically rewritten
External catalog tables: partition evolution is controlled by the external catalog; Snowflake writes follow the current spec

snowflake:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution support via Snowflake clustering mechanisms for V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
Partition evolution managed through Snowflake clustering

snowflake:multi-arg-transforms:v2.notes: Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:multi-arg-transforms:v3.notes: Multi-argument transforms support not explicitly mentioned in the V3 GA announcement. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
Not explicitly mentioned in announcement

snowflake:time-travel:v2.notes: Time travel is fully supported for Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables. For external catalog tables, time travel depends on periodic metadata refresh completing before snapshots expire.
caveats: Full time travel support for Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables
For external catalog tables, time travel depends on periodic refresh completing before snapshot expiry

snowflake:time-travel:v3.notes: Time travel fully supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:table-maintenance:v2.notes: Snowflake automatically handles table maintenance for managed Iceberg tables including auto-compaction, manifest compaction, and snapshot expiry. Orphan file deletion is not supported. As of September 2025, compaction can be disabled per-table via ENABLE_DATA_COMPACTION=false (account, database, schema, or table level). As of October 2025, a target file size for Snowflake-managed tables can be set to control compaction output.
caveats: Automatic compaction of small data files (enabled by default; disable with ENABLE_DATA_COMPACTION=false)
Automatic manifest compaction
Automatic snapshot expiry
Set target file size for compaction output (GA October 2025)
Orphan file deletion is NOT supported

snowflake:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Automatic table maintenance supported for Iceberg V3 tables including compaction, manifest compaction, and snapshot expiry. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026). Compaction can be disabled via ENABLE_DATA_COMPACTION=false and a target file size for compaction output can be configured.
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
Automatic compaction enabled by default; disable with ENABLE_DATA_COMPACTION=false
Target file size for compaction output configurable (GA October 2025)
Orphan file deletion is NOT supported

snowflake:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support Iceberg branching and tagging
caveats: Iceberg branching and tagging features are not available in Snowflake

snowflake:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Snowflake does not support Iceberg branching and tagging for V3 tables.
caveats: Not mentioned in V3 GA announcement

snowflake:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support for Iceberg tables in Parquet format. Supports both Snowflake-managed and externally managed Iceberg tables.
caveats: Only Parquet file format is supported for Iceberg tables
Supports reading both Snowflake-managed and externally managed Iceberg tables

snowflake:read-support:v3.notes: Full read support for Iceberg V3 tables, both Snowflake-managed and externally managed. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:write-insert:v2.notes: INSERT INTO is fully supported for both Snowflake-managed and externally managed Iceberg v2 tables. Write support for externally managed tables (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE) is generally available as of October 2025. External writes commit changes back to the linked external REST catalog. Partitioned writes (GA October 2025) ensure partition-spec-compliant data layout for both table types.
caveats: Fully supported for Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables
Write support for externally managed tables (GA October 2025): INSERT supported for tables linked to external Iceberg REST catalogs (AWS Glue, Snowflake Open Catalog, Unity Catalog, etc.)
Partitioned writes (GA October 2025): Snowflake writes data conforming to the table's Iceberg partition spec
CREATE ICEBERG TABLE … AS SELECT is not supported with vended credentials for catalog-linked databases

links.label: Write support for externally managed tables (GA Oct 2025)
Write support for externally managed tables (docs)

snowflake:write-insert:v3.notes: Full write (INSERT) support for Iceberg V3 tables, including streaming and batch ingestion. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: MERGE, UPDATE, and DELETE are fully supported for both Snowflake-managed and externally managed Iceberg v2 tables. Write support for externally managed tables (GA October 2025) enables full DML operations that commit changes back to the linked external REST catalog. Snowflake-managed v2 tables default to copy-on-write; externally managed v2 tables default to merge-on-read with positional delete files (ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR='AUTO').
caveats: Snowflake-managed v2: all DML operations use copy-on-write by default (rewrite affected data files)
Externally managed v2: DML defaults to merge-on-read with positional delete files (GA October 2025)
Write mode can be controlled via ICEBERG_MERGE_ON_READ_BEHAVIOR parameter
Snowflake uses heuristics (row %, file size) to switch between MoR and CoW per file when MoR is active

links.label: Write support for externally managed tables (GA Oct 2025)
Write support for externally managed tables (docs)

snowflake:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: Full MERGE/UPDATE/DELETE support for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Snowflake supports a broad set of catalog integrations: Snowflake itself (managed tables), REST Catalog (generic), AWS Glue, Databricks Unity Catalog, Snowflake Open Catalog (Polaris), Google BigLake Metastore (GA Jun 2, 2026), Microsoft Fabric / OneLake (GA Jan 30, 2026), and Delta Sharing. Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 external volumes are GA (Jun 12, 2026).
caveats: Supports: Snowflake-managed, AWS Glue, Unity Catalog, Open Catalog (Polaris), REST generic, BigLake Metastore (GA Jun 2026), Fabric/OneLake (GA Jan 2026)
Azure DLS Gen2 external volumes: GA June 12, 2026

snowflake:catalog-integration:v3.notes: V3 support integrated with external catalog integrations. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:hive-metastore:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support Hive Metastore as a catalog for Iceberg tables
caveats: Hive Metastore is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Snowflake does not support Hive Metastore for Iceberg
caveats: Hive Metastore is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: Snowflake supports AWS Glue as an external catalog for Iceberg tables. Read access via external tables and write support via REST catalog integration.
caveats: Read access to Iceberg tables registered in AWS Glue Catalog
Write support available via REST catalog integration

snowflake:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: AWS Glue Catalog supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:rest-catalog:v2.notes: Snowflake supports the Iceberg REST Catalog protocol for both consuming tables from external catalogs and exposing Snowflake-managed tables to external engines (Horizon Catalog). Supported REST catalogs include Open Catalog (Polaris), Google BigLake Metastore (GA Jun 2, 2026), Microsoft Fabric / OneLake (GA Jan 30, 2026), and any generic REST catalog server. Private connectivity for catalog-vended credentials is GA (Jun 2, 2026).
caveats: Supports any REST Catalog-compliant server for reading and writing externally managed tables
Horizon Catalog exposes Snowflake-managed tables via REST Catalog API for external engines
Google BigLake Metastore via REST catalog integration: GA Jun 2, 2026
Microsoft Fabric / OneLake via REST catalog integration: GA Jan 30, 2026
Private connectivity for catalog-vended credentials: GA Jun 2, 2026

snowflake:rest-catalog:v3.notes: REST Catalog supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:nessie:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support Nessie as a catalog for Iceberg tables
caveats: Nessie catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:nessie:v3.notes: Snowflake does not support Nessie catalog
caveats: Nessie catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:polaris:v2.notes: Snowflake Horizon Catalog is built on the open-source Polaris project, providing native Polaris catalog integration.
caveats: Snowflake Horizon Catalog is Snowflake's managed service built on Polaris
Native integration with full read and write support

snowflake:polaris:v3.notes: Snowflake Horizon Catalog (Polaris) supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Full read and write support for Databricks Unity Catalog managed Iceberg tables. Write support is GA on AWS (Oct 2025) and Azure (Apr 2026, requires Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 external volume). CREATE ICEBERG TABLE … AS SELECT (CTAS) is also GA (Mar 2026). Snowflake commits all DML changes back to Unity Catalog as the remote catalog.
caveats: Write support on AWS: GA since October 2025
Write support on Azure: GA since April 2026 (requires Azure DLS Gen2 external volume)
CTAS (CREATE ICEBERG TABLE … AS SELECT) for Unity Catalog: GA since March 2026
Uses REST Catalog API integration with Databricks Unity Catalog

links.label: Write support for Unity Catalog on Azure (GA Apr 2026)

snowflake:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Full read and write support for Unity Catalog managed Iceberg V3 tables. All V3 capabilities (deletion vectors, row lineage, variant type, etc.) are supported. Write support on AWS and Azure is GA. Generally available as of May 7, 2026.
caveats: Generally available as of May 7, 2026
Write support on Azure requires Azure DLS Gen2 external volume

links.label: Write support for Unity Catalog on Azure (GA Apr 2026)

snowflake:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support Hadoop catalog for Iceberg tables
caveats: Hadoop catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: Snowflake does not support Hadoop catalog
caveats: Hadoop catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support JDBC catalog for Iceberg tables
caveats: JDBC catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: Snowflake does not support JDBC catalog
caveats: JDBC catalog is not a supported catalog integration

snowflake:table-creation:v2.notes: Snowflake supports creating Iceberg tables as Snowflake-managed tables (using an external volume or Snowflake-managed storage), or as externally managed tables linked to an external catalog. As of June 1, 2026 (GA), you can create Iceberg tables using Snowflake storage — no external volume required, with Fail-safe protection and transient table support. A DEFAULT_METADATA_WRITE_FORMAT account/database/schema parameter (GA June 2026) allows creating Iceberg tables via standard CREATE TABLE without the ICEBERG keyword.
caveats: Snowflake-managed tables: CREATE ICEBERG TABLE with an external volume (S3, Azure, GCS), or with Snowflake storage (no external volume, GA Jun 1, 2026)
Snowflake storage option: Fail-safe protected, transient variant available; commercial AWS and Azure regions only
Externally managed tables: linked to AWS Glue, Unity Catalog, BigLake Metastore, Fabric/OneLake, or generic REST catalog
DEFAULT_METADATA_WRITE_FORMAT parameter (GA Jun 5, 2026): set account/DB/schema default to create Iceberg tables without ICEBERG keyword

links.label: Snowflake storage for Iceberg tables (GA Jun 1, 2026)

snowflake:table-creation:v3.notes: Full table creation support for Iceberg V3 tables, both Snowflake-managed and externally managed. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:variant-type:v2.notes: Variant Type is a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:variant-type:v3.notes: Variant data type supported in Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:shredded-variant:v2.notes: Shredded Variant is a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:shredded-variant:v3.notes: Shredded variant support not explicitly mentioned in the V3 GA announcement. Variant type is supported. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)
Not explicitly mentioned in announcement

snowflake:geometry-type:v2.notes: Geometry Type is a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:geometry-type:v3.notes: Geography and Geometry types supported in Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:lineage:v2.notes: Lineage is a V3 feature not available in Iceberg V2

snowflake:lineage:v3.notes: Row-level lineage tracking supported for data governance and auditing in Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

links.label: Use row lineage with Iceberg tables

snowflake:statistics:v2.notes: Snowflake writes column-level statistics (min/max values, null counts, value counts) to Iceberg manifest files for managed tables and uses them for scan planning and file pruning.
caveats: Statistics are managed automatically by Snowflake for managed Iceberg tables
Externally managed tables retain statistics written by the originating engine

snowflake:statistics:v3.notes: Column-level statistics supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:bloom-filters:v2.notes: Snowflake does not support Iceberg bloom filters (Parquet-level or Puffin-based) for Iceberg tables. Snowflake uses its own micro-partition pruning and search optimization instead.
caveats: Snowflake uses proprietary micro-partition pruning instead of Iceberg bloom filters

snowflake:bloom-filters:v3.notes: Snowflake uses proprietary micro-partition pruning instead of Iceberg bloom filters.
caveats: Snowflake uses its own pruning mechanisms

snowflake:type-promotion:v2.notes: Snowflake supports type promotion for Iceberg tables in accordance with the Apache Iceberg spec: int to long, float to double, and increasing decimal precision. Available via ALTER ICEBERG TABLE ALTER COLUMN SET DATA TYPE.
caveats: Only supported for Snowflake-managed Iceberg tables, not externally managed tables
Metadata-only operation; existing data files are not rewritten

links.label: ALTER ICEBERG TABLE SET DATA TYPE

snowflake:type-promotion:v3.notes: Type promotion supported for Iceberg V3 tables. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
caveats: Generally available (as of May 7, 2026)

snowflake:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: Snowflake Horizon Catalog is Snowflake's managed Iceberg REST Catalog built on Apache Polaris. External query engines (Spark, Trino, DuckDB, PyIceberg, Flink, etc.) can connect via a single REST endpoint. Read support GA Feb 6, 2026. Write support for external engines GA May 26, 2026. Masking policies and row access policies defined in Snowflake are enforced when Spark queries tables through Horizon Catalog (Jan 2026). Works with both tables on external volumes and Snowflake-managed storage (GA Jun 1, 2026). Available on commercial AWS and Azure regions.
caveats: External engine READ via Horizon Catalog: GA since Feb 6, 2026
External engine WRITE via Horizon Catalog: GA since May 26, 2026
Security policy enforcement (masking + row access policies) via Spark: GA Jan 27, 2026
Snowflake storage for Iceberg tables (no external volume needed) accessible via Horizon Catalog: GA Jun 1, 2026
Available in commercial AWS and Azure regions; GCP not yet supported for Snowflake storage
API requests billed at 0.5 credit per million calls (billing starts second half of 2026)

links.label: Access Iceberg tables with external engines via Horizon Catalog
External query engine read support (GA Feb 2026)
Enforce data protection policies via Spark (Jan 2026)
Snowflake storage for Iceberg tables (GA Jun 2026)

snowflake:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: Full Iceberg V3 support via Snowflake Horizon Catalog for external engines, including deletion vectors, row lineage, and variant types. Read and write via external engines GA. Generally available as of May 7, 2026.
caveats: Generally available as of May 7, 2026
External engine read and write via Horizon Catalog supported for V3 tables

links.label: Access Iceberg tables with external engines via Horizon Catalog

snowflake:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

snowflake:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: Deletion vectors replace position deletes in V3, providing improved write performance for row-level deletes. Generally available (as of May 7, 2026).
